title: aglaia tomentosa subsp. kabaensis
intro: Aglaia tomentosa subsp. kabaensis is a subspecies of plant in the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the Kaba Aglaia or the Kaba tree. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Aglaia tomentosa subsp. kabaensis include its potential in treating fever, headache, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as an anti-aging agent due to its antioxidant properties. Aglaia tomentosa subsp. kabaensis can be prepared as a herbal remedy through various methods, including decoction, infusion, and extraction, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and other medicinal products.
